issue_body:
### Describe the bug

After upgrading from 3.13.5 to 3.14.0 (no other dependency changes), we started seeing read timeouts on requests that reuse pooled keep-alive connections.

The key tell that this is a poisoned-connection problem and not a genuinely slow read: the "read timeout" fires almost immediately (~2ms), not after our configured sock_read of 3s. A real read timeout would take ~3s to surface. A ~2ms failure means the connection handed out of the pool was already broken / desynchronized before the first read on it.

Pinning aiohttp==3.13.5 (and nothing else) eliminates the failures.

### To Reproduce

Our setup: aioboto3 / aiobotocore (which uses aiohttp as its HTTP transport) talking to Amazon DynamoDB. DynamoDB closes idle keep-alive connections after ~20 s of inactivity, so pooled connections routinely go stale (server-side FIN/RST) while parked in the pool.

The conditions are:

1. A long-lived ClientSession with keep-alive pooling and ClientTimeout(sock_read=3) (a total timeout reproduces it too).
2. A pooled connection that DynamoDB has already closed after >20s idle, and/or a request cancelled mid-response under concurrency.
3. On reuse, the read against that connection is interrupted. In 3.13.5 the transport was closed and the bad connection evicted, so the next request acquired a fresh one. In 3.14.0 the connection appears not to be closed and is returned to the pool, so subsequent requests keep picking it up and failing in single-digit milliseconds (~2 ms, versus the configured 3s sock_read) instead of recovering.

A server that drops idle keep-alive connections on a short timer, like DynamoDB's ~20s.

### Expected behavior

A connection whose read/parse was interrupted by cancellation should be closed and discarded (3.13.5 behavior), not released back into the keep-alive pool, so subsequent requests don't inherit a broken connection.


### Logs/tracebacks

Traceback (most recent call last):

botocore.exceptions.ReadTimeoutError: Read timeout on endpoint URL: "https://dynamodb.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/"

### Python Version

```console
$ python --version
Python 3.13.13
```
